summary: Frontend-only regression for iter-114.11 ruler stacked-assembly bugfix. Focused test on the reported user issue (cone-on-cube ruler unresponsiveness). All four in-scope checks PASS: (P0) ruler origin ↻/× buttons remain responsive after repeated snap-dot picks across a stacked cube+cone assembly; (P1) snap-dot toggle add→remove logic; (P1-b) origin action buttons visible + clickable when the origin sits directly on the cube corner geometry; (P2) placing the ruler on empty bed still commits. DOM elementFromPoint at ↻ centre returns the BUTTON itself (data-testid='workplane-ruler-replace') — validating zIndexRange=[9999,9990] and confirming no other Html overlay is intercepting the click.

test_results.P0_stacked_assembly_ruler_still_responsi: PASS — After adding a cube (id cube-…, dims 20³, pos [0,0,10]) and a cone (dims r10 h24, pos [0,0,32]) on top, clicked workplane-ruler-toggle-btn → placing=true + workplane-ruler-placing-hint visible. Placed ruler at [10,-10,0] via store.placeWorkplaneRuler. Confirmed workplane-ruler-replace (↻) and workplane-ruler-remove (×) render at 24×24 px (6×6 tw = 24 px, matches the size bump in the fix). Added 3 sequential picks: cube corner [10,10,20], then switched selection to cone and added [0,0,40], then [5,5,40] — each replaced the previous per single-pick semantics. After these picks, elementFromPoint at ↻ centre returns {tag:'BUTTON', testid:'workplane-ruler-replace'} — NO other overlay occludes the button. Clicking ↻ (force=True) transitioned store to {active:false, placing:true} and re-showed the placing-hint banner. Clicking × after re-placement transitioned to {active:false, placing:false} — ruler removed. This exactly reproduces the user's failing scenario and confirms the priorityRaycast + zIndexRange fix works.
test_results.P1_snap_dot_toggle: PASS — addRulerPick([10,10,20]) yielded rulerPicks.length=1; then executing SnapDots' toggle-match logic (find existing pick within 0.01 mm and call removeRulerPick) yielded rulerPicks.length=0. Add→same-point→remove cycle verified.
test_results.P1b_origin_buttons_over_cube_corner: PASS — With ruler origin at [10,-10,0] (front-right-bottom cube corner), both workplane-ruler-replace and workplane-ruler-remove remained is_visible() = true. The Html overlay for the buttons is centred on the origin sphere and only translates (+24 px, -24 px) via CSS, so the buttons sit slightly above/right of the geometry and are unoccluded.
test_results.P2_bed_placement_smoke: PASS — Clicked workplane-ruler-toggle-btn, workplane-ruler-placing-hint appeared, canvas click at empty bed area (20% left, 78% bottom) transitioned store to {active:true, placing:false, origin:[-59.5,-122.8,0]}. workplane-ruler-placer-plane raycast against Z=0 hit and committed correctly.
test_results.console_health: Only pre-existing cosmetic WebGL 'GPU stall due to ReadPixels' driver warnings (identical to iter-113/iter-114 baseline). NO new JS errors or React error boundaries during the ruler flow.

critical_code_review_comments: priorityRaycast (WorkplaneRulerPicks.jsx line 34) uses distance = -1e-4 to force front-of-queue ordering. Correct approach — three.js sorts intersects ascending on `distance`, so any positive-distance mesh's hit will land after. Note: R3F's onClick fires for the FIRST intersect only when stopPropagation() is called; without stopPropagation R3F walks the ordered list. The snap-dot handler DOES call e.stopPropagation(), so front-of-queue = handler wins. Good.

Potential subtle issue: priorityRaycast is exported from WorkplaneRulerPicks.jsx and imported by WorkplaneRuler.jsx. If a future refactor renames or re-locates WorkplaneRulerPicks the import chain breaks silently at runtime — origin sphere loses raycast override. Consider hoisting priorityRaycast into a shared file like /app/frontend/src/lib/threeHelpers.js.

The WorkplaneRuler.jsx origin sphere is rendered on TOP of a smaller inner ring (line ~246). BOTH now use priorityRaycast, so a ray always hits the OUTER sphere first (larger radius = earlier ray entry). The inner ring's priorityRaycast is defensive (in case its z-order is flipped by renderOrder=1003 > 1002). Nice defence in depth.

downPosRef in WorkplaneRuler.jsx tracks pointer-move distance for drag-vs-click discrimination but the click/drag branching has been *replaced* by the explicit ↻ button (iter-114.10). The downPosRef.moved flag is now dead state — never read. Consider removing the block on lines ~80–84 to reduce cognitive load. Non-blocking.

PickMeasurement chip (WorkplaneRulerPicks.jsx line ~181) uses a hard-coded transform 'translate(80px, -110px)' relative to the pick point. On a very small viewport or when the pick is near the top-right of the canvas the chip could be clipped. Consider clamp-to-viewport (out-of-scope for this bugfix).

SnapDots renders 14 spheres per selection (8 corners + 6 face centres). Each attaches an onClick + onPointerDown + onPointerOver + onPointerOut. That's 56 handlers per selection change — cheap for 14, but if a future feature adds edge midpoints back (27-point grid) it grows to 27*4 = 108. Non-issue today.

Radius bump 1.8 → 2.6 mm is a UX win but shifts the click target ratio between corner (yellow) and face-centre (purple) dots. Both use the same 2.6 mm sphere; visually indistinguishable at zoom. Could differentiate radius by kind (e.g., corners 2.6, face 2.0) if users report accidental face-centre picks. Non-blocking.
test_credentials: Fresh seeded MongoDB session_token=st_iter115_1783033094186 (user t115@example.com) — valid ~7 days. Injected via Playwright cookie for domain orca-cad-slice.preview.emergentagent.com. Prior iter-114 token (st_iter114_1782699840835) is expired.
seed_data_creation: Transient scene only: window.__forgeStore.getState().addPrimitive('cube') + addPrimitive('cone') + updateObject(coneId, position:[0,0,32]) to stack them. Not persisted (no /api/me/designs write). Session doc is the only DB row created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ruler UI: click workplane-ruler-toggle-btn to enter placing (banner workplane-ruler-placing-hint appears), next canvas click commits via placeWorkplaneRuler. Origin ↻ button = workplane-ruler-replace, × = workplane-ruler-remove — both HTML overlays sitting above the R3F canvas via drei <Html zIndexRange={[9999,9990]}>. Snap-dot picks are R3F meshes (14 per selection: 8 corners yellow #F59E0B, 6 face-centres purple #C084FC) — cannot be driven reliably via Playwright synthetic clicks (documented in iter-113); use window.__forgeStore.getState().addRulerPick([x,y,z], {snapKind,objId}) to simulate. Toggle-off logic lives in SnapDots.onClick (WorkplaneRulerPicks.jsx line ~112). The exposed dev hook is window.__forgeStore (Zustand store). Note: seeding raw object literals into window.__forgeStore.setState({objects:[…]}) will crash Inspector because Inspector expects fields (size arrays) that only buildPrimitive() attaches — always use addPrimitive() for test scenes.
rca of the issue: Original bug (fixed pre-testing by main agent): (a) snap-dot meshes and ruler-origin sphere had depthTest:false so they appeared visually 'on top', but three.js raycast sorts hits by geometric distance — for stacked geometry (cone on cube) the parent cube mesh was hit first, its onClick called e.stopPropagation, and the ↻/× buttons + snap-dots never received the click. Fix: custom priorityRaycast() forces the target mesh's hit distance to -1e-4 so it always sorts FIRST regardless of stacked geometry. (b) The Html overlay for the origin action buttons used a lower zIndexRange; bumping to [9999,9990] guarantees no other Html overlay (e.g., pick chips at [71,0]) occludes them. Mitigation verified: after 3 sequential picks across cube+cone, elementFromPoint at ↻ centre returned the button itself (not any occluding element), and clicking it correctly re-entered placing mode.
